item.title: Drivers winched to safety after ignoring road closures in WA's flooded Gascoyne following Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Authorities say motorists are repeatedly ignoring warnings not to drive in floodwaters in WA's north in the wake of Cyclone Narelle, with 20 people having to be rescued.

item.title: National campdraft competition postponed over soaring cost of fuel
item.description: One of Australia's biggest campdrafting events is postponed due to the fuel crisis, with organisers hoping to reschedule the event within a few months.

item.title: Anthony Albanese calls for 'more certainty' on US objectives in Iran war
item.description: Anthony Albanese wants "more certainty" from the United States on its war objectives in Iran and says the administration must be clear if it is still pursuing regime change.

item.title: Australia's four-step plan to weather the international fuel crisis aims to protect supply and keep country moving
item.description: Fuel could be rationed and funnelled towards critical sectors such as emergency services under worst-case-scenario planning for shortages caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Star Manly recruit Jamal Fogarty labels sacking of ex-coach Anthony Seibold 'pretty disappointing'
item.description: Jamal Fogarty finds it hard to believe that the man who convinced him to join the Sea Eagles has been shown the door after just three games working together this season.

item.title: No clear evidence ANU's plan to cut jobs and courses was needed, unreleased audit office draft says
item.description: ANU's council approved a massive $250 million cost-cutting program without clear evidence it was needed or achievable, according to an assessment in a draft audit office report.

item.title: Romanian trio charged accused of stealing wallets from elderly shoppers in Queensland
item.description: Police allege three Romanian nationals would monitor victims, some aged between 70 and 94, as they used ATM machines or paid for groceries.

item.title: WA parliamentary inquiry into greyhound racing begins as animal welfare advocates call for sport to be banned
item.description: A former greyhound racing insider tells how he reached "breaking point" with the industry, as a parliamentary inquiry into the sport in WA begins.

item.title: Kangaroos' Tristan Xerri suspended for three matches after rubbing blood on Essendon captain's face
item.description: Kangaroos ruckman Tristan Xerri gives evidence at the AFL tribunal as he accepts a suspension for rubbing blood from his nose on the face of Essendon captain Andrew McGrath.

item.title: Economists warn cutting fuel excise could keep inflation higher for longer
item.description: Drivers will pay about 26.3 cents less on every litre of fuel they buy for three months, but some economists warn it could ultimately leave Australians worse off.

item.title: Jaycob Gemza was drinking, on phone when ute hit Bryson Dimovski, court hears
item.description: Bryson Dimovski, 14, was left on an embankment for six hours after Jaycob Gemza fatally struck him in Newcastle in 2023, a court has been told.

item.title: From tax cuts to price gouging crackdowns, how G7 nations are combating the fuel crisis
item.description: Australia is not alone in weathering rising fuel costs and issues with domestic supply and distribution amid the war in the Middle East. G7 nations are also working out ways to ease the crisis.

item.title: Is AI going to cause a job wipeout in the future?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Fuel excise halved for three months
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Sharon Fulton's killer Raymond Reddington sentenced to life in prison
item.description: Raymond Reddington is sentenced to life in prison with a minimum of 20 years for murdering Sharon Fulton in Perth in 1986, as her family continues to search for "her final resting place".

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Kiera Austin creates period shorts, Kate Moloney collision raises safety questions and Mary Cholhok must lift
item.description: World Cup-winning Diamond Kiera Austin remembers having to learn to use a tampon earlier than she was ready in order to play netball. Now she's developed a period undershort she hopes will address the alarming rates of Australian teens dropping out of sport.

item.title: Five social media platforms investigated over compliance with under-16 ban
item.description: Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are being investigated for potential noncompliance with the ban, almost four months after the laws came into force.

item.title: Myanmar's junta chief Min Aung Hlaing steps down to pursue presidency
item.description: Myanmar's military chief has stepped down to seek the presidency in the general's latest efforts to maintain power behind the facade of a civilian administration.

item.title: China keeps Iranian oil moving through Hormuz as war reshapes trade
item.description: China is now the world's largest crude oil importer, and Iran — though a discounted and often opaque supplier — remains a consistent part of that supply mix.

item.title: If your home is underinsured it comes with big risks, experts say
item.description: If your home is underinsured, it means your insurance policy won't fully cover the cost of rebuilding, repairing or replacing it.

item.title: Bittersweet Iraqi film The President's Cake brings hope as war rages in the Middle East
item.description: Filmmaker Hasan Hadi had to bring flowers to school to celebrate Saddam Hussein’s birthday. That memory loosely informs his beautiful debut feature, The President’s Cake.

item.title: Insiders profit as Iran war pressure points build on global economy
item.description: Global stock markets, which for the past month have been remarkably restrained as the Middle East crisis unfolded, have begun to wobble.

item.title: Fuel purchasing powers could be used for nearly anything facing supply disruption
item.description: There are concerns future governments could abuse broad powers that are designed to support companies to buy more fuel but could be used on any good facing supply-chain disruption.

item.title: Early government modelling assumes fuel rationing when stockpile falls to 10 days' diesel supply
item.description: Diesel stocks would last about six months if Australia's imports dropped by 20 per cent, according to modelling compiled for the federal government early in the Middle East war.

item.title: 50 per cent of Australians avoid health care they need due to cost, according to Consumers Health Forum report
item.description: One in two Australians missed out on health care they needed last year mainly because they could not afford it, according to a new report by the peak healthcare consumers group.

item.title: How one man's stolen identity was used by the man accused of running an international cult from Nannup
item.description: In mid-2009 British citizen Simon Kadwill-Kelly was catching a ferry returning from France. Within minutes of trying to board he found out his identity had been stolen.

item.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent
item.description: The last of the 48 teams for the FIFA World Cup will be decided tomorrow, with Italy hoping to end a 12-year exile, while Türkiye and Kosovo look to join the Socceroos' group.

item.title: The Matildas hype is real. So why are the A-League Women crowds still so small?
item.description: Despite the recent hype of the Matildas, support for the A-League Women remains relatively low.

item.title: Hobart City Council to introduce safety liaison officers following rise in antisocial behaviour
item.description: A trio of council-employed public safety monitors will soon be patrolling "hot spots" in the Hobart CBD, to try to curb antisocial behaviour and theft.

item.title: The oil shock has completely reshaped Australia's petrol price cycle
item.description: Petrol stations in our biggest cities hiked prices ahead of global oil prices, but experts say their profit margins are now much slimmer.

item.title: The 'miracle' drugs giving children with spinal muscular atrophy another shot at life
item.description: Children born with a rare neuromuscular disorder are being given another chance in life thanks to a new treatment experts are calling a "game-changer".

item.title: Passengers fear V/Line trains already hitting capacity as free travel begins
item.description: Commuters say they are worried overcrowding on V/Line train services could worsen as free travel begins for Victorians to ease the pain of the fuel crisis.

item.title: From Missy Higgins to a succulent Chinese meal, these are the 2026 Sounds of Australia
item.description: A variety of iconic Australian recordings are added to the Sounds of Australia collection, as part of an annual update by the National Film and Sound Archive.

item.title: Coalition grills PM over fuel crisis during Question Time — as it happened
item.description: Anthony Albanese faces a grilling from MPs as energy minister provides fuel availability update.

item.title: Amid fuel price rises, business owners plead for kindness as abuse from customers increases
item.description: From pile-on comments on social media to roadside slurs, local businesses are reminding their communities to "be kind to the little guy" amid the fuel crisis.

item.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi half-century leads Rajasthan Royals to IPL win over Chennai Super Kings
item.description: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashes a 15-ball half-century as he leads Rajasthan Royals to an easy victory over Chennai Super Kings.

item.title: Chicken and eggs could end up cheaper because of new high-protein sorghum variety
item.description: Australian researchers develop a high-protein sorghum variety, hoping to replace expensive livestock feed with systems that could lead to cheaper meat and eggs.

item.title: Northern Territory paramedics face record rates of assault amid critical resourcing shortage
item.description: The ABC rode along with St John Ambulance NT paramedics to hear the impact under-resourcing is having on the workforce, including stories of staff being assaulted on the job.

item.title: Hydrostor energy project and stargazing tourism business resolve dispute over outback skies
item.description: The battery project, which will provide back-up power for up to 80,000 homes in Broken Hill, will go ahead with new conditions after a dispute was resolved between the company and an outback stargazing business.

item.title: Federal Court sitting on a Kimberley cattle station labelled as 'history making'
item.description: It was a court hearing unlike any other, complete with country music, "killer" beef, and gaggles of kids and dogs dashing between the black-clad lawyers.

item.title: RBA to remove surcharges on debit, credit cards, card networks including eftpos, Mastercard and Visa
item.description: The removal of surcharges on debit and credit cards is one of the sweeping reforms from the Reserve Bank.

item.title: Heavy rain, floods kill 45 people in Afghanistan, Pakistan as fighting continues
item.description: Heavy rain has triggered severe flooding and caused buildings to collapse, killing 45 people and injuring 74 in Afghanistan and Pakistan over the past five days, with Kabul also warning of continued risks from bad weather as clashes with Pakistan continue.

item.title: Man charged over Narooma pub shooting, armed robbery and carjacking
item.description: Police have arrested and charged 41-year-old Deon Rodney Mason over the shooting and alleged armed robbery at a pub on the NSW far south coast.

item.title: Anonymous $200,000 donation comes at the right time for a Central Coast charity
item.description: The cash donation alongside a free van will help the charity continue to feed people in need and allow it to expand its service.

item.title: Warren Purse says paramedics in the NT are under pressure
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tim Rogers on making a living, fatherhood and a You Am I classic
item.description: The frontman of ARIA Hall Of Famers You Am I on work, family, and why Hourly, Daily is still special to him.

item.title: Is this image of a child climbing on a missile real? ABC NEWS Verify explains
item.description: Huge amounts of false images and video circulating about the conflict in Iran are often making it difficult to distinguish genuine news from manipulated fakery, but this one is real.

item.title: Dezi Freeman hid at a property perfectly designed for someone wanting to live off-grid
item.description: The rural property where police finally caught up with the fugitive gunman was a good place to disappear.

item.title: Telstra defends coverage after new rules for mobile coverage maps introduced
item.description: ACMA has introduced new rules for standardised mobile coverage maps that will see mobile signals below -115 dBm labelled as "no coverage".

item.title: Man accused of murdering sex worker at Melbourne brothel ordered to face trial
item.description: Michael James Chalmers is ordered to face trial after pleading not guilty to murdering a sex worker at a Melbourne brothel in 2024.

item.title: Fuel crisis puts pressure on workers, business, as calls to work from home reignite
item.description: The fuel crisis is putting pressure on workers who rely on car travel and those who meet with vulnerable clients, with fresh calls for frontline support staff to be exempt from any future fuel rationing mandates.

item.title: Young farmers embracing latest agricultural technology with drones and apps
item.description: A shift towards modern technology is making a career in agriculture more appealing to a new generation of farmers.

item.title: Brooke Watts using the ag-tech staple, a drone
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bell frogs vaccinated against chytrid fungus released in Sydney Olympic Park
item.description: Chytrid fungus has been linked to the extinction of 90 frog species around the world. In a long-abandoned Sydney brick pit, a new front has opened in the fight to save an iconic species.

item.title: Salary dramas, blown draft picks and scandals — How Essendon and Carlton found themselves in AFL purgatory
item.description: Essendon and Carlton were once mainstays when it came to the business end of the AFL season. Over the last quarter century, both clubs have fallen on hard times. Here's why.

item.title: NT government revives 1940s law in bid to combat fuel price gouging
item.description: The Northern Territory government has triggered a decades old law in an attempt to combat fuel price gouging at the bowser.

item.title: Public hearing into hiring of new CIT CEO cancelled as probe into process continues
item.description: The chair of the CIT board told an ACT Legislative Assembly inquiry the complaints made against chief executive Margot McNeill related to perceptions of conflicts of interest around employments and contracts.

item.title: NSW premier calls for more sovereignty on national energy supply as petrol prices rise
item.description: NSW Premier Chris Minns calls for greater fuel security while unveiling the state's fuel savings measures, arguing for more electric vehicles and charging stations.

item.title: With the fuel excise halved, when will petrol and diesel prices drop in Australia?
item.description: Australians should save about 26c a litre at the pump after the federal government announces a cut to the fuel excise.

item.title: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle brings floods to WA cattle stations after years of drought
item.description: West Australian pastoralists have faced severe flooding after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle wreaked havoc in one of the state's most important food-producing regions.

item.title: Tributes for farming stalwart Ian Burnett and grandson after farming accident
item.description: Tributes are flowing in for a central Queensland producer who died with his grandson when machinery rolled on a property at the weekend.

item.title: New research finds Tasmanians are 40 per cent more likely to die from MND
item.description: Tasmanian grandmother Kelli Skipper had to travel for hours to get the medical treatment she needed after being diagnosed with a form of motor neurone disease. She is now calling for change.

item.title: Liam Alexander Hall may enter insanity plea over alleged terrorism act at Perth Invasion Day rally
item.description: A man accused of tossing a homemade "fragment bomb" filled with ball bearings and screws into a crowd in Perth's CBD flags through his lawyer that he may argue he is not criminally responsible for his actions due to insanity.

item.title: Queensland boaties in stand-off with stowaway sugar glider discovered sailing in Moreton Bay
item.description: The nocturnal marsupial, which typically spends the day sleeping in tree hollows, was discovered when the sails of the boat were opened.

item.title: 'Welcome to Country' pioneer farewelled on her beloved Bundjalung Country
item.description: Rhoda Roberts AO is being remembered for her ability to laugh and embrace life, as well as her contribution to the Australian arts and Indigenous cultural movements.

item.title: Why these women have never had a partner
item.description: Three women share their experiences of never having a partner — not by choice — and what that's like when you wish you did.

item.title: Iron ore miner Fenix Resources came within days of running out of fuel as Middle East war prompts rethink
item.description: Junior West Australian iron ore miner Fenix Resources is forced to scale back non-essential activities to keep its operations running after coming within days of running out of fuel.

item.title: Carlie Richards sentenced after fleeing crash which killed Erica Hoy at Gillman
item.description: A woman who was found to be mentally incompetent when she caused a fatal multi-car crash has been released into a transitional mental health facility.

item.title: Australian Antarctic Division's cultural workplace reforms yield mixed results
item.description: Workplace reforms implemented by the Australian Antarctic Division (AAD) after two damning reports into the organisation's culture have led to some improvements but a review has found problems persist.

item.title: Zac Lomax available to make Super Rugby Pacific debut if selected for Western Force
item.description: Zac Lomax could get his first taste of rugby union at a venue where the Western Force recruit is no stranger to receiving a hostile welcome.

item.title: SA electoral problems under scrutiny as ex-Liberal MP reveals he received extra upper house ballot
item.description: As authorities commit to a review of problems faced by South Australian voters during this month's election, a former Liberal MP says he was concerned to received an extra ballot paper in the post.

item.title: Melbourne couple allegedly beat and starved woman enslaved in their home, court hears
item.description: A court has heard an Indonesian woman was allegedly threatened and coerced to provide domestic services while living with a Melbourne couple.

item.title: Tourists told to stay away from cyclone-ravaged Exmouth, Coral Bay and Onslow
item.description: Famed holiday hotspots in Western Australia's north, including Exmouth and Coral Bay, are reluctantly turning away tourists ahead of the Easter holiday peak as they rebuild in the wake of Cyclone Narelle.

item.title: Victims of Ponzi scheming Mildura accountant lament 'manifestly inadequate' sentence
item.description: An accountant from Mildura in Victoria's north-west found guilty of an egregious breach of trust has avoided a lengthy prison term, to the dismay of his clients.

item.title: WA man Samuel Jingle sentenced to life in prison after killing woman in state's north
item.description: Samuel Jade Lincoln Jingle will have to serve a minimum of 19 years in prison after killing his partner in Fitzroy Crossing.

item.title: Devastated artists rally after Daly River floods destroy Merrepen Arts Centre
item.description: Artists from the remote Northern Territory community of Nauiyu are facing a devastating loss after the Merrepen Arts Centre was destroyed by recent flooding.

item.title: NSW government's proposed removal of 'good character' evidence could undermine justice, legal experts say
item.description: Legal figures are pushing back against sweeping changes to New South Wales sentencing laws, warning proposed reforms to abolish "good character" references could undermine long-standing principles of justice.

item.title: Inquest into Mackay grandmother's death hears of 'shortcomings' in private hospital's after-hours staffing
item.description: A coroner finds the post-surgery care of Robyn Beale at Mater Private Hospital Mackay was not "adequate or appropriate", but the inquest heard the 78-year-old would have died regardless.

item.title: Israel's ambassador defends new death penalty for Palestinians
item.description: Hillel Newman uses an address to the National Press Club to defend Israel's war with Iran and its military action in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Barbie fans paid hundreds of dollars for a festival in Florida. Here's why they weren't impressed
item.description: Barbie fans who paid up to $656 for an "unforgettable experience" were met with a concrete-floored warehouse and flimsy pink cardboard cut-outs.

item.title: Thousands of Chinese social media users change profile photos to Kris Jenner
item.description: Thousands of Chinese social media users have started using images of US celebrity Kris Jenner as their profile photos in hopes it will bring them success and good fortune.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Dezi Freeman's nephew Luke Filby speaks out about his 'evil' uncle's death at hands of Victoria police
item.description: When Dezi Freeman was shot dead, it left his family with mixed emotions. Some are shattered, others know an "evil man" is dead. His nephew speaks out.

item.title: Socceroos thump Curaçao 5-1 in World Cup farewell, as Irankunda electrifies
item.description: Nestory Irankunda scores twice as the Socceroos' substitutes spark a 5-1 win over Curaçao in their FIFA Series clash in Melbourne.

item.title: The final hours of Dezi Freeman
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Gangland violence now hurting the innocent
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: WA's greyhound racing regulator defends track record at inquiry into sport's future
item.description: The body governing greyhound racing in WA says it is determined to maintain the welfare of both the dogs and the industry at a parliamentary inquiry, which was sparked by a petition that garnered 26,000 signatures.

item.title: Police charge 13yo girl after alleged crime rampage in Melbourne
item.description: Victoria Police say the teenager was driving a stolen car when she swerved towards a group of people from the Jewish community on Saturday.

item.title: House prices rise for first quarter of 2026, but 'widening divergence' between capital cities paints future picture
item.description: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Japan deploys first long-range missiles in southwest region near China
item.description: The upgraded Type-12 missile has a range of about 1000 km, allowing it to now reach mainland China.

item.title: King Charles confirms US state visit amid growing European tensions over Iran war
item.description: US President Donald Trump lashed out at the UK just before the visit was confirmed as France, Spain and Italy restrict airspace to US war planes.

item.title: Donald Trump slams 'very unhelpful' France, UK as tensions with allies intensify over Iran war
item.description: The US president again attacks America's allies over their reluctance to get involved in the Iran war, warning them the "US won't be there to help you anymore, just like you weren't there for us".

item.title: The rugged country that holds the secrets of Dezi Freeman's final days
item.description: The fugitive resurfaced more than 150 kilometres away from the town where he shot and killed two police officers seven months ago. Mystified locals are questioning how.

item.title: Travel quiz, March 29: Get out your passport and see if you can beat our quiz
item.description: Which is Australia’s oldest Luna Park? And where would you find Elvis Presley’s mansion?

item.title: Is E10 fuel bad for my car? And could it save me money?
item.description: Australia's leading motoring organisation is urging drivers to fill up with E10 fuel in an effort to conserve national fuel supplies. But what exactly is E10? And could it help ease the current fuel crisis?

item.title: The Drama, starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, is a dark rom-com with a controversial twist
item.description: On the surface, A-listers Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new film seems like a regular rom-com. That's exactly what director Kristoffer Borgli wants you to think.

item.title: Calls intensify for heli-tourism proponent's lease at Lake Malbena to be cancelled
item.description: A private developer who wants to build a standing camp on an island in the Tasmanian Wilderness World Heritage Area tells the ABC he has requested another lease extension, amid an outcry from the Tasmanian Greens.

item.title: Asia to get its own edition of Eurovision this year with Bangkok to host grand final
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest will launch its first-ever Asia edition this year, with Bangkok set to become the first host city for the 10-country competition.

item.title: Experts say trigger for fuel rationing is not a 'precise' date
item.description: The federal government is not expected to publicly nominate a clear trigger point for fuel rationing, despite internal modelling outlining potential thresholds.

item.title: The government says it is 'situation normal' but are we already in a recession?
item.description: The government is urging people to carry on as normal, despite fuel shortages, because it knows that panic will make matters worse. But it may need to consider more drastic economic intervention.

item.title: Dezi Freeman joins a long history of Australian fugitives captured or killed
item.description: Australia has a long history of violent outlaws heading bush to avoid capture. But how is it still possible to remain on the run for so long?

item.title: Why your household could be hit by a bill hike trifecta this month
item.description: Millions of Australian households will be hit with a trifecta of price hikes this month. Here's what to know about the changes and how they could affect you.

item.title: Regional Australians disappointed by Vodafone ad claiming 'nothing's out here'
item.description: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

item.title: Sydney developer who illegally cut down native trees lodges plan for $10m mansion
item.description: A property developer who illegally cut down hundreds of native trees has lodged a new plan to build a lavish $10 million, six-bedroom mansion in Sydney's south.

item.title: 80,000 Australian kids aged five and under not up to date with vaccinations
item.description: The latest immunisation data shows more than 80,000 Australian children aged five and under are not fully up to date with their vaccinations.

item.title: Scientists discover more than 117 deep-sea creatures in Coral Sea
item.description: More than 110 new fish and invertebrate species are discovered during a CSIRO voyage in the deep Coral Sea.

item.title: Free dental clinic helps Adelaide residents access care in cost-of-living crisis
item.description: Demand is high for an Adelaide dental program that offers free services to patients who have experienced homelessness or who have trouble accessing conventional care.

item.title: Israeli death penalty law discriminatory and may constitute 'war crime', says UN
item.description: UN rights chief Volker Turk calls for the bill to be "promptly repealed", warning it is "patently inconsistent with Israel's international law obligations".

item.title: Queensland retailer Sabo Skirt accuses Shein, Kmart and other businesses of duplicating designs
item.description: Queensland business Sabo Skirt has claimed Shein, Kmart and numerous other retailers and wholesalers have copied its clothing patterns, prints and trademarks on "lower quality" garments in a federal court claim.

item.title: Socceroos headliners shine, but will all of Tony Popovic's experimentation pay off at the World Cup?
item.description: The Socceroos are done with experimenting, but now need to find the winning formula with the World Cup quickly approaching.

item.title: Live: Albanese promises to do 'everything' to protect Australians from worst of war
item.description: He said Australians can expect the economic shock from the war in the Middle East to last ‘months’. Follow live.

item.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Iran sets 24-hour deadline on threat of strikes against US tech companies
item.description: US companies including Microsoft, Google and Apple could be targeted, while Donald Trump urges the world to buy American fuel.

item.title: Qld's hospitals tackle health of patients and planet in emission reduction efforts
item.description: The WHO lists climate change as the single biggest health threat facing humanity, responsible for the deaths of 13 million people annually. Now, Queensland hospitals grapple with reducing their own environmental footprint.

item.title: Government deadline for gun reform passes with less than half states and territories signed up
item.description: The federal government's March deadline for national gun reform has come and gone, with more than half the states and territories still opposed to parts of the plan. Only four have committed to a gun buyback scheme.

item.title: Tiger Woods to seek treatment after pleading not guilty to DUI following car crash
item.description: Tiger Woods says he will step away and seek treatment, days after he was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence.

item.title: Bikie and money laundering claims taint Hellyer mine as government moves to shut it down
item.description: The Tasmanian government has taken steps to revoke the operating licence of one of the state's biggest mining operations, after being warned it was allegedly used as a "vehicle for money laundering" and former owners were associated with underworld figures.

item.title: 'Giveaway' club loses licence after multiple breaches of law
item.description: RS Rewards claims to have given away more than $10 million worth of cars, houses and cash. But its licence has just been cancelled.

item.title: Lebanon's Beirut airport operating despite Israeli strikes and closed airspaces
item.description: Beirut-Rafic Hariri Airport, Lebanon's gateway to the world, is still operating even as Israel bombards its surroundings.

item.title: ASX jumps on back of massive Wall St rally amid war end hopes — as it happened
item.description: Wall Street ends a weak month with a massive gain, as investors eye a possible end to US military action in Iran, and Australian shares follow.

item.title: Cocoa prices plummet but Easter chocolate stays expensive
item.description: Consumers cannot expect to find cheaper chocolate on supermarket shelves this Easter despite a major drop in the world cocoa price, industry experts say.

item.title: Emerson Jones named in Australia's squad for Billie Jean King Cup tie against Great Britain
item.description: At just 17, Emerson Jones is a chance to make her Billie Jean King Cup debut after being named in Australia's squad to face Great Britain.

item.title: Judge halts Donald Trump's $400m White House demolition and ballroom project
item.description: A federal court has ordered the Trump administration to suspend work on a $400 million ballroom on the site of the demolished East Wing of the White House, barring construction from proceeding without congressional approval.

item.title: Thermal imaging and night-time shooting key to Kangaroo Island corella control, authority says
item.description: Thermal imaging, infrared technology and crack marksmanship are deployed to battle the island's growing little corella population.

item.title: From screen to symphony: Joff Bush on Bluey’s new orchestral album and music from the hit series
item.description: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

item.title: Internal memos show NT government warned of 'significant risks' in watch houses
item.description: Internal government memos obtained by the ABC detail a series of health incidents in Northern Territory watch houses and a suicide attempt in the Holtze prison.

item.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
item.description: Police in Iraq say they are searching for an American journalist who has been kidnapped in Baghdad, possibly by an Iranian-backed militia group.

item.title: The list that helps Aussie surfing world champion Molly Picklum handle the noise in her head
item.description: Reigning world surfing champion Molly Picklum kicks her off title defence this week at Bells Beach, where writing a list may form part of her preparation.

item.title: Australia's Cooper Connolly makes stunning IPL debut with Punjab Kings
item.description: Cooper Connolly puts in a game-winning performance for Punjab in his IPL debut.

item.title: Droving, selling or putting cattle on a boat, this is how NSW farmers are navigating the drought
item.description: Farmers are turning to roadside grazing, selling up stock, and, in one case, shipping cattle to an island to navigate the drought and the rising cost of diesel.

item.title: NT tourism operators facing hundreds of thousands in damage costs after multiple devastating weather events
item.description: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

item.title: Italy fails to reach third straight World Cup after penalty shootout loss to Bosnia and Herzegovina
item.description: Italy's World Cup exile continues after the four-time champions fall to a 4-1 penalty shootout defeat by Bosnia and Herzegovina.

item.title: Teenager paralysed in hay bale accident still determined to farm
item.description: A Queensland teenager paralysed after a hay bale accident on his family farm is determined to return to the land despite the challenges of life in a wheelchair.

item.title: Man accused of setting body on fire after alleged murder at Dimbulah in Far North Queensland
item.description: A 37-year-old man has been arrested and charged with murder a week after burning human remains were found at a Dimbulah property.

item.title: NFL's first female official Robin DeLorenzo accuses league in lawsuit of being 'a sexist institution unable to treat a woman as an equal'
item.description: One of the first female officials to be hired by the NFL, Robin DeLorenzo, says that she suffered "open hostility" and "gender-based scrutiny" during her three years as an official in the league.

item.title: Seafood industry urges public to buy local for Easter as fuel costs bite
item.description: Seafood operators are struggling to stay afloat, with the nation's biggest wild-caught tuna and swordfish company confirming it is shutting up shop.

item.title: Seafood giant A Raptis and Sons to close after administrators fail to find buyer
item.description: Australia's largest wild-caught prawn operation is to close its doors after 60 years in business, impacting hundreds of workers and devastating a Queensland community.

item.title: Man sentenced to four years' prison for stabbing wife to death outside Darwin hotel
item.description: Dennis Naroldol, 59, has been sentenced to four years in prison over the fatal stabbing of his wife outside the Darwin DoubleTree Hilton in 2023 and will be eligible for parole in two weeks after time served.

item.title: Fuel crisis driving calls for better public transport in regional Western Australia
item.description: Busselton has just three regular bus services, leaving 85-year-old Jan Whalan with an hour's trip on her mobility scooter to get to the shops — but as the fuel crisis also bites, there are calls for better public transport.

item.title: Tottenham fan groups protest Roberto De Zerbi appointment over Mason Greenwood support
item.description: Tottenham fan groups voice opposition to newly appointed manager Roberto De Zerbi over his previous public support for Mason Greenwood, a player who faced charges of attempted rape and assault.

item.title: Anthony Albanese to urge Australians to 'play their part' in Iran response in TV address
item.description: The prime minister's office says Anthony Albanese will address the nation on TV tonight, urging Australians to save fuel for areas and industries that "most need it".

item.title: An 'ebullient' feeling as markets price in an end to the Iran war, but there's a catch
item.description: If you had woken up today and heard someone say Wall Street's tech sector rose more than 3 per cent overnight, you would expect it to be promptly followed by "April Fools' Day".

item.title: Victoria Police formally identify Dezi Freeman as man shot dead in Thologolong
item.description: Victoria Police have officially confirmed that the person shot dead by officers at a property in Victoria's north-east on Monday morning was fugitive Dezi Freeman.

item.title: Taylor Swift faces trademark lawsuit over The Life of a Showgirl album
item.description: Las Vegas performer Maren Wade says the name and imagery of Taylor Swift's latest album are too close to those of her show, Confessions of a Showgirl.

item.title: Court told Newcastle man Anthony Nugent shot dead for selling fake gun
item.description: The Supreme Court has heard Newcastle man Anthony Nugent was shot dead at his front door as payback for selling a fake gun.

item.title: Sam Konstas left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for 2026-27 season
item.description: Sam Konstas is one of six players left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for the upcoming season.

item.title: GOAT is Australian artist Daniel Pozo's first project as a Sony lead
item.description: Daniel Pozo has had a hand in Sony hits like Spiderman: Across the Spider-Verse and Hotel Transylvania, but GOAT is his first project in the driver's seat.

item.title: Elderly man accused of killing fellow care home resident allowed to remain in care as case progresses
item.description: Alan William Williams, 78, died after an alleged altercation with resident Thomas Raymond Pryce, 84, at the Regis aged care centre in Aspley last month.

item.title: Fears for superb fairy-wrens as experts studying Canberra population predict extinction within decades
item.description: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

item.title: Mouse plague warning for Western Australia as researcher urges action
item.description: Grain paddocks in Western Australia are filled with thousands of mice which are making their way into residential areas.

item.title: Trump says work on 'massive' White House bunker to go ahead despite block on ballroom
item.description: US President Donald Trump says work will continue on underground bunker at the White House after a federal judge ordered him to suspend his ballroom project, which demolished the building's East Wing.

item.title: Australia assessing how to protect Pacific nations from fuel shortages caused by Iran war
item.description: Pacific nations are not yet suffering large-scale petrol shortages but are already starting to grapple with the economic impacts of the war, with fuel price hikes threatening to drive up inflation.

item.title: Police find $320,000 worth of allegedly stolen Lego, charge two people
item.description: A man and woman will face court after allegedly stealing hundreds of Lego sets from retail outlets across metropolitan Adelaide.

item.title: Fuel prices have begun to drop across capital cities as fuel excise cut kicks in today
item.description: Consumers were warned the fuel excise cut might take weeks to be passed on to retailers, but prices have already begun to fall, NRMA says.

item.title: Zac Lomax to make Super Rugby debut for Western Force in Brisbane
item.description: Zac Lomax will make his highly anticipated Super Rugby debut this weekend, almost a month to the day after his hopes of playing in the NRL this season were ended.

item.title: Bobby Weaver pleads guilty to murder of man buried inside freezer in Ipswich backyard
item.description: Bobby Weaver pleaded guilty to murder more than seven years after the death of 58-year-old David Thornton, whose body was found buried in a freezer in his Ipswich backyard.

item.title: Australians expected to generate more solar panel waste each year by 2030, inquiry hears
item.description: Australians are expected to throw away 90,000 tonnes of solar panel waste each year by 2030, a federal government hearing has been told.

item.title: Algal bloom tracks further north up the Yorke Peninsula, sparking concern for stingrays
item.description: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

item.title: Cyclist's death deemed 'foreseeable' after warning about dangerous tree went unaddressed, coroner finds
item.description: A cyclist’s death on a popular Canberra path could have been prevented if the ACT government had acted promptly on a public warning about a dangerous overhanging branch, coronial findings reveal.

item.title: Australians reveal toll of fuel price rises on their lives as cost of living goes up
item.description: The ABC asked how you were coping with fuel prices and here's what we heard back.

item.title: March brought a rare blood moon, a rarer cyclone to WA — see WA's best weather photos
item.description: Nature put on a special show for Western Australia in March, including a total lunar eclipse and rough seas from a powerful tropical cyclone, in addition to the spectacular sunsets the state is known for.

item.title: What to know about the four Artemis II astronauts heading to the Moon
item.description: The Artemis II mission to the Moon could take four astronauts further from Earth than any human has gone before. Here's what we know about them.

item.title: Conservation group plans to replant Daintree Rainforest trees on former sugarcane land
item.description: A conservation group's plans to replant species from the World Heritage-listed Daintree Rainforest on land used for growing sugar have not won everybody over.

item.title: There's only been a handful of national addresses. Anthony Albanese has now made another
item.description: Pandemics, wars, and landmark moments have been immortalised in addresses to the nation. Anthony Albanese has become one of the few prime ministers to employ the form.

item.title: Murder charge against partner of Mullumbimby's Ashleigh Grice is dropped
item.description: A murder charge has been withdrawn against a Mullumbimby man over the death of his partner, Ashleigh Grice, after a post-mortem examination found the 23-year-old woman died of "mixed drug effects".

item.title: Court makes landmark decision after illegal logging in NSW state forest
item.description: Traditional owners say a $450,000 fine will not replace the six centuries-old giant trees illegally felled by NSW Forestry Corporation.

item.title: Daniel's Law child sex offender register website receives more than 290,000 visits in three months
item.description: Queensland's first public child sex offender register, listing reportable offenders who have failed to comply with their reporting obligations or whose whereabouts are unknown, has been praised for protecting children.

item.title: Former University of Sydney academic appears in Iranian propaganda video
item.description: An Australian academic has appeared in an Iranian propaganda video supporting a toll system imposed by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on foreign merchant shipping.

item.title: Kieran Foran adamant age is not an issue ahead of first match as Manly Sea Eagles coach
item.description: Kieran Foran says he will not be afraid to have hard conversations with former teammates as he prepares to complete the fastest shift from NRL player to head coach this century.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos senior leaders decider to stand down winger Josiah Karapani
item.description: Brisbane captain Adam Reynolds says the club's senior leaders took a non-negotiable stance on culture and behaviour to stand down winger Josiah Karapani for one match.

item.title: Track the latest petrol and diesel prices around Australia
item.description: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

item.title: Graham Arnold coaches Iraq to first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years
item.description: Former Socceroos coach Graham Arnold has guided Iraq to their first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years with a playoff win over Boliva.

item.title: Regional Victorian businesses struggling to stay afloat amid rate rise, petrol prices
item.description: Regional businesses buckle under rising costs, saying the economic situation is even worse than during COVID.

item.title: Tuesday no longer cheapest day for petrol in Perth as Middle East war disrupts fuel price cycle
item.description: Perth motorists can no longer rely on "cheap Tuesday" fuel prices and are urged to shop around every day as the Middle East war disrupts global supply chains.

item.title: Non-profit aged care hit with 6,000 per cent rent hike by NSW Crown Lands
item.description: An aged care provider in Sydney's north says a move by NSW Crown Lands has unfairly targeted them in an attempt to maximise returns for the taxpayer.

item.title: WA government to use emergency powers to force fuel companies to reveal supply chain information
item.description: The Western Australian government will use emergency powers to force fuel companies to provide information about their supply chains in an effort to keep fuel flowing through the state.

item.title: Years after devastating floods, residents watch as former homes demolished
item.description: Former residents of an Ipswich unit complex repeatedly damaged by floods have watched as the buildings are destroyed for good.

item.title: Murder trial begins over 2024 death of Alice Springs teenager Yiel Gatluak
item.description: Jurors in the trial for the alleged 2024 murder of Yiel Gatluak in Alice Springs have heard they will see "a degree of violence most of you probably won't be accustomed to".

item.title: Anthropic boss makes big call on Australian copyright as artists say pay up
item.description: The AI company's billionaire boss says he is not trying to convince Australia to change its mind on protecting the copyright of artists.

item.title: Victim speaks out after sexual assaults on remote farm in north-east Victoria
item.description: A sexual assault survivor says the sentence handed to a well-known farmer and former community leader who groped and kissed her on his remote Victorian property is disappointing.

item.title: Former University of Wollongong chief John Dewar grilled by inquiry about KordaMentha contract
item.description: Former interim vice-chancellor John Dewar says he can see a "perception" of conflict of interest after a $3.8m contract was awarded to his consultancy firm by the University of Wollongong.

item.title: Jury shown crucial exhibits on final day of evidence in Irma Palasics cold case murder trial
item.description: A milk container and water jug carrying DNA evidence were revealed to jurors as a decades-old murder trial reached its final stage.

item.title: Class action alleges GrainCorp had 'expand first, fix problems later' approach to production
item.description: A class action of 18 people from the small Victorian town of Numurkah alleges that agribusiness giant GrainCorp's increased production at the site has led to offensive noise and odour for residents.

item.title: Live Nation's Love Song 2026 finds gen Z embrace retro tech, scrapbooking, in-person connection
item.description: Gen Z is embracing retro technology like iPods, vinyl and Polaroid cameras and craves in-person connection more than any other generation, according to a Live Nation study.

item.title: Queensland's first recovery village for eating disorder survivors opens on Sunshine Coast
item.description: A couple whose daughters survived eating disorders is opening a first-of-its-kind recovery village to help survivors ease back into their normal lives without relapsing.

item.title: Wednesday finance with
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: NBL owner Larry Kestelman prepared to walk away from Wilkinsons Point land purchase
item.description: After the Tasmanian government was urged to abandon the sale of prime waterfront land but chose to persevere, it now appears the developer is prepared to "walk away" from the potential purchase.

item.title: Albanese urges Australians to 'live life as normal' during fuel crisis in rare national address
item.description: Anthony Albanese asks Australians to trade their cars for public transport if they can, while reassuring those who need to drive they can continue to do so amid the global oil crisis.

item.title: Anthony Albanese delivers address to the nation on fuel crisis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'I understand that right now it's hard to be positive': Anthony Albanese address
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Victorian public schools are underfunded and face teachers leaving the state for higher salaries
item.description: Victoria bills itself as the 'education state' but a growing exodus of teachers and underfunding has that tag under threat.

item.title: Australian rugby union coach Mick Byrne ends tenure as Fiji boss just 18 months out from World Cup
item.description: Mick Byrne steps down from his role as head coach of the Fiji national rugby team after two years in charge.

item.title: Anthony Albanese's national address contained reassurance on the fuel crisis — and a warning shot
item.description: The prime minister risks criticism for using the rare measure of a national address to urge calm without announcing any new measures.

item.title: Michael Voss says Carlton hurting but committed to improving against North Melbourne
item.description: Carlton head coach Michael Voss has said there is not a "silver bullet" to fix the club's second-half blues, following three successive fade-outs to start the season.

item.title: Teens arrested over multiple break and enter offences
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Five teenagers arrested after multiple home invasions across Brisbane and Gold Coast
item.description: Multiple people were injured during the series of break-and-enter offences across South Brisbane and the Gold Coast.

item.title: Prime Minister urges Australians to save fuel
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Staff accused of stealing $1 million from Village Roadshow
item.description: Village Roadshow allege more than $1 million in inappropriate payments had already been identified "with the total amount likely to increase as the investigation continues".

item.title: Global uncertainty a reason to 'press ahead' with urgent reform, Albanese says
item.description: The prime minister will use a speech at the National Press Club to make his pitch for "urgent" change amid significant international uncertainty caused by the Middle East war.

item.title: Adelaide 36ers force NBL title decider in Game 4 cliffhanger against Sydney Kings
item.description: Torrey Craig's missed free throw for Sydney in the dying seconds allows Adelaide to level the NBL championship series at 2-2 with a gripping 92-91 win.

item.title: WA man charged with terrorism offences reflected on Bondi attacks in diary, court told
item.description: Jayson Joseph Michaels, 20, had reflected on the Bondi attacks in his diary and was allegedly planning a "day of justice", a Perth court hears.
